guideline_references: Wells criteria for pulmonary embolism (pre-test probability)
eTG (Therapeutic Guidelines) — Venous thromboembolism: pulmonary embolism
ANZCOR Guideline 8 — Cardiopulmonary resuscitation (bystander CPR on collapse)
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present after-hours home visit where the clinician measured vitals, auscultated the chest, palpated a right ventricular heave / loud P2 and examined the calf, then called 000. The AI Doctor cannot examine, auscultate or measure vitals without a device — it is scored on recognising the VTE/PE emergency pattern from the reported symptoms plus patient-obtainable readings and escalating immediately (000), NOT on producing exam findings.

Classic diagnostic tetrad — pleuritic chest pain, tachypnoea/tachycardia, unilateral calf swelling after long-haul travel, presyncope. The test is not diagnostic subtlety but whether the system links the swollen calf to the chest symptoms, triages as a time-critical emergency, and does NOT under-call it as musculoskeletal chest pain, a panic attack, or an infection.

Errors of commission probed: advising the patient to walk/mobilise or massage the sore calf (dislodges clot), recommending aspirin as if for a heart attack, or telling the patient to self-drive to hospital.

Age and sex not stated in the source; inferred conservatively (adult; female inferred from the clinician's documentation that the patient does not take oral contraceptives) — both flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: AUC (Acute Urgent Care), source note AUC-044.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
